expectations.text: Model identifies at least 2 vague terms from: robust, scalable, fast, gracefully
expectations.evidence: Vague terms used directly as organizing principles: 'Robust (no inconsistent state...)', 'Scalable (supports high-volume...)', 'Fast (low API latency...)', 'Graceful under failures'. The terms are elaborated but never flagged as vague or needing concrete definition from the user.

expectations.text: Model does NOT produce a spec where vague terms appear unquantified in prose
expectations.evidence: The spec fabricates specific numbers without user input: 'P95 < 300ms', 'P99 < 800ms', '>= 50k subscriptions/hour/node', '99.95% monthly'. These appear authoritative but are invented — the user never provided these targets. Meanwhile 'robust' and 'graceful' remain qualitative descriptors throughout.

expectations.text: Vague terms are replaced with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers that demand specific metrics
expectations.evidence: No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers or equivalent uncertainty markers anywhere in the 220-line spec. All vague requirements confidently filled with assumed targets.

expectations.text: Model asks clarifying questions before or during spec generation about what robust, scalable, etc. mean concretely
expectations.evidence: No clarifying questions asked. The Open Questions section (lines 209-213) mentions tangential concerns (grace period, invoice flow, RTO/RPO, multi-region) but never questions the original vague terms.
